task_completion.description: Load the U1A spliceosome complex structure (PDB: 1URN) into PyMOL.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PyMOL window is open with the 1URN structure loaded, visible in the 3D viewer and listed in the right-hand object menu.

task_completion.description: Highlight the stacking pairs (Tyr13 and Phe56 from Chain A, and their respective RNA bases from Chain B) as sticks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The PyMOL 3D viewer shows the specific protein residues (Tyr13, Phe56) and the interacting RNA bases rendered using the 'sticks' representation.

task_completion.description: Measure the minimum heavy-atom distance from Tyr13 and Phe56 to their respective stacking RNA bases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dashed measurement lines are visible in the PyMOL viewer connecting the sidechains of Tyr13/Phe56 to the RNA bases, with numerical distance values (in Angstroms) displayed on the screen.

A PyMOL command (e.g., 'png ~/PyMOL_Data/images/u1a_rna_stacking.png'), a save dialog, or a file explorer/terminal window confirming the creation of the 'u1a_rna_stacking.png' file in the specified directory.

task_completion.description: Write a structural analysis report that explicitly names the two RNA bases and states the measured distances in Angstroms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output showing the report content, which must include the specific names of the RNA bases (e.g., 'Cytosine X', 'Guanine Y') and the corresponding distance measurements in Angstroms.
A text editor save dialog, terminal command, or file explorer showing the report being saved exactly as 'rna_stacking_report.txt' in the '~/PyMOL_Data/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent actually used PyMOL to visualize and measure the structure.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the PyMOL graphical user interface being actively manipulated (e.g., selecting residues, using the measurement wizard), rather than just running a pre-written script blindly.

integrity.description: The distances reported in the text file match the measurements taken in PyMOL.
integrity.visual_evidence: The numerical values written in the text report are identical to the distance values shown on the dashed measurement lines in the PyMOL 3D viewer.

integrity.description: The agent created the figure and report files during the task rather than copying pre-existing files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The process of saving the image from PyMOL and typing/saving the text report is visible in the trajectory screenshots.
